volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: The Rope (Section 5.3.1415b - Section 5.3.1423c)

sections.label: Section 5.3.1415b
sections.original: Perii hercle . nisi me suspendo , occidi .
sections.translation: Troth , I ’ m undone ; if I don ’ t hang myself [*] , I ’ m utterly done for . ( Aloud .) I ’ faith , after this day you certainly shall never be cheating me again .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1417b
sections.translation: Be it so ; the proposal is to my taste .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1418
sections.original: Sequimini intro . spectatores , vos quoque ad cenam vocem ,
sections.translation: Do you both follow me in-doors . ( He comes forward and addresses the AUDIENCE .) Spectators , I would invite you to dinner as well , were it not that I ’ m going to give nothing , and that there is no good cheer at all at home ;

sections.label: Section 5.3.1420
sections.original: nive adeo vocatos credam vos esse ad cenam foras .
sections.translation: and if , too , I didn ’ t believe that you are invited to dinner elsewhere . But if you shall be willing to give hearty applause to this Play , do you all come to make merry at my house some sixteen years hence . Do you ( to LABRAX and GRIPUS ) both dine here with me to-day .
